user_message: Can you extract the partner engagement from this support ticket for Tailspin Toys? This is a renewal account with declining consumption.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-18 17:30 EST
Subject: Follow-up: partner engagement Review

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with partner engagement. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
Date: 2024-02-18 09:10 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Tailspin Toys partner engagement

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with partner engagement. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-25 10:17 EST
Subject: Re: partner engagement Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for partner engagement. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Tailspin Toys: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

handoffs.condition: contact and opportunity records need updating

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF38-D trap (Opportunity-at-risk flag missed when champion held the deal); produces complete Stakeholder map delta, opportunity-at-risk assessment, re-engagement plan, contact update proposals,
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF38-D failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF38-D failure mode (Opportunity-at-risk flag missed when champion held the deal)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct partner engagement decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF38-D -- Opportunity-at-risk flag missed when champion held the deal
